title: Palicourea acicularis
intro: Palicourea acicularis, also known as "Palicourea", is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Palicourea acicularis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiproliferative activities. In traditional medicine, Palicourea is often prepared as an infusion or a decoction to treat various ailments, and it is also used to make topical ointments and creams for skin conditions.
